[newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Ska+jazz%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Ska+jazz%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Ska+jazz%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Ska+jazz%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(September 2012)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

is a [music genre](/wiki/Music_genre "Music genre") derived by fusing the [melodic](/wiki/Melody "Melody") content of [jazz](/wiki/Jazz "Jazz") with the [rhythmic](/wiki/Rhythm "Rhythm") and [harmonic](/wiki/Harmony "Harmony") content of early [Jamaican Music](/wiki/Music_of_Jamaica "Music of Jamaica") introduced by the "Fathers of Ska" in the late 1950s. The ska-jazz movement began during the 1990s in New York and London, where pioneering [avant-garde jazz](/wiki/Avant-garde_jazz "Avant-garde jazz") and reggae musicians pushed the boundaries of reggae music. They were combining traditions with modern tendencies, using the reggae beat along with high improvisation and jazz harmonies, primarily by horns and percussion.
Barton, Paul Alfred, *Americans and Their Idols: The Return of Soul, Style, Country, Pop, Poetry*. Infinity Publishing, 2006, p. 1.
Bogdanov, Vladimir, [*All Music Guide to Soul: The Definitive Guide to R&B and Soul*](https://books.google.com/books?id=o552g5xRRiwC), Hal Leonard Corp., 2003.

The ska-jazz movement began during the 1990s in New York and London, where pioneering [avant-garde jazz](/wiki/Avant-garde_jazz "Avant-garde jazz") and reggae musicians pushed the boundaries of reggae music. They were combining traditions with modern tendencies, using the reggae beat along with high improvisation and jazz harmonies, primarily by horns and percussion.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Music genre

| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Ska jazz"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Ska+jazz%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Ska+jazz%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Ska+jazz%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Ska+jazz%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Ska+jazz%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Ska+jazz%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(September 2012)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

| Ska-Jazz | |
| --- | --- |
| Stylistic origins | [Ska](/wiki/Ska "Ska"), [jazz](/wiki/Jazz "Jazz") |
| Cultural origins | Late 1950s, Jamaica |

**Ska jazz** is a [music genre](/wiki/Music_genre "Music genre") derived by fusing the [melodic](/wiki/Melody "Melody") content of [jazz](/wiki/Jazz "Jazz") with the [rhythmic](/wiki/Rhythm "Rhythm") and [harmonic](/wiki/Harmony "Harmony") content of early [Jamaican Music](/wiki/Music_of_Jamaica "Music of Jamaica") introduced by the "Fathers of Ska" in the late 1950s. The ska-jazz movement began during the 1990s in New York and London, where pioneering [avant-garde jazz](/wiki/Avant-garde_jazz "Avant-garde jazz") and reggae musicians pushed the boundaries of reggae music. They were combining traditions with modern tendencies, using the reggae beat along with high improvisation and jazz harmonies, primarily by horns and percussion.

## Origins

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ska_jazz&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Origins")]

The term Ska-Jazz was coined by Rock Steady Freddie (Fred Reiter) of the New York Ska-Jazz Ensemble in 1994. Ska jazz is sometimes considered a [subgenre](/wiki/Subgenre "Subgenre") of [third wave ska](/wiki/Third_wave_ska "Third wave ska"), but early artists such as Tommy McCook considered themselves jazz musicians foremost. Before Rock Steady Freddie, Ska-Jazz was just another flare of jazz without its own name. Jazz improvisation is commonly used in Ska jazz. Ska Jazz bands usually contain one or two [electric guitars](/wiki/Electric_guitar "Electric guitar"), a [bass guitar](/wiki/Bass_guitar "Bass guitar"), [keyboards](/wiki/Keyboard_instrument "Keyboard instrument"), a [drum kit](/wiki/Drum_kit "Drum kit") and a [horn section](/wiki/Horn_section "Horn section") (composed of any combination of the following: [trumpet](/wiki/Trumpet "Trumpet"), [trombone](/wiki/Trombone "Trombone"), [alto saxophone](/wiki/Alto_saxophone "Alto saxophone"), [tenor saxophone](/wiki/Tenor_saxophone "Tenor saxophone"), and [baritone saxophone](/wiki/Baritone_saxophone "Baritone saxophone")). Occasionally there may be one or more [vocalists](/wiki/Vocalist "Vocalist"), but the genre is primarily focused on [instrumental](/wiki/Instrumental "Instrumental") tunes. The [brass instruments](/wiki/Brass_instrument "Brass instrument") usually carry the melody, and there are many [improvised](/wiki/Musical_improvisation "Musical improvisation") solos. The rhythm section places accents on the [off beats](/wiki/Off-beat_(music) "Off-beat (music)"), thus giving the music a different feel than straight jazz.

Ever since its birth in the late 1950s, ska has been a genre marked by physical and cultural diasporas and an openness to borrowing from outside its origins.[[1]](#cite_note-1) The history of ska and jazz combined travels across national borders and integrates with other musical styles, making it one of the most hybrid, transnational forms of postwar popular music.[[2]](#cite_note-2)
